name: codex-memory-provider-benchmark-v1
description: Semantic retrieval benchmark for standalone codex-memory embedding providers.

documents.text: The standalone Codex memory MCP server exposes a memory overview tool that summarizes audit logs, recall activity, shadow sync health, and adapter status.
After a diary write succeeds, the system dual-writes into SQLite shadow storage and the vector index. If a side write fails, it records a reconcile task instead of rejecting the diary entry.
The candidate cache stores ranked chunk candidates and is invalidated whenever knowledge base sync detects updates or stale shadow entries.
Context vectors blend active blocks, optional context text, and recent conversation history into the search query representation before retrieval.
Provider-aware rerank adapters support Cohere, Voyage, Jina, and generic endpoints with different request payloads and paths.
External embedding adapters support Cohere, Voyage, and Jina APIs while falling back to local hash embeddings when remote providers are unavailable.
Diary enhanced vectors summarize the process and knowledge notebooks and add notebook-level semantic routing bias during candidate scoring.
The provider smoke CLI verifies remote embedding and rerank connectivity from environment variables and prints a JSON or text report.

queries.query: How can I inspect audit logs and recall health for Codex memory?
What happens if the side index write fails after the diary entry is already saved?
Which component reuses ranked retrieval candidates until the knowledge base changes?
How does retrieval inject recent conversation state into the query embedding?
Which providers have dedicated reranking request formats in this project?
Which remote services are supported for real embedding generation?
What adds notebook-level semantic bias during candidate generation?
Which command checks remote provider connectivity and prints a report?
